name: Salesforce, Inc.
shortDescription: The #1 CRM software company, evolving its multi-cloud Customer 360 suite into an AI 'digital labor' platform (Agentforce + Data 360).
businessModel: Recurring per-seat SaaS subscriptions (~95% of revenue) plus an emerging Agentforce consumption (Flex Credits) motion; multi-cloud land-and-expand.
marketContext: Largest enterprise applications vendor; the bellwether for whether incumbent software can monetize AI agents faster than AI compresses the per-seat model.

leadership.name: Marc Benioff
leadership.title: Chair of the Board & Chief Executive Officer (Co-Founder)
leadership.background: Co-founded Salesforce in 1999; pioneer of cloud SaaS.

leadership.name: Robin Washington
leadership.title: President & Chief Operating and Financial Officer
leadership.background: Appointed Feb 2025; former Gilead CFO. Also principal accounting officer as of March 2026.

leadership.name: Srinivas (Srini) Tallapragada
leadership.title: President & Chief Engineering and Customer Success Officer

leadership.name: David Schmaier
leadership.title: President & Chief Strategy Officer
leadership.background: Former Chief Product Officer; founder of Vlocity (acquired by Salesforce).

rampDemandSignal.aiSectorContext: Technology-sector AI adoption rose 70.4% (Jun 2025) -> 77.2% (Apr 2026) across 50k+ businesses

freshnessNote: All multiples reflect the 2026-06-05 market close.

trading.evBasis: recomputed: last close × shares + net debt

plain: Salesforce makes the software companies use to keep track of their customers — every sales call, support ticket, marketing email and online order — so a business has one shared view of who its customers are and what they need.
It rents that software to ~150,000 companies as an annual subscription. Its newest push, 'Agentforce,' adds AI agents that can actually do customer-service and sales work, not just store the data.
technical: Multi-tenant SaaS: all customers run on one shared codebase and metadata layer spanning Sales, Service, Marketing/Commerce, Slack/Platform, Tableau, MuleSoft, Data 360 and Agentforce.

Revenue is ~95% recurring subscription & support, billed annually in advance — which is why RPO ($67.9B) and cRPO ($33.6B, +14%) are the key forward indicators. Agentforce layers a consumption model (Flex Credits, ~$0.10/action) on top of per-seat licensing, expanding TAM from human seats toward enterprise labor budgets.
The model throws off enormous cash: 34.8% non-GAAP operating margin and $6.6B of free cash flow in a single quarter (Q1 FY27).
simple: Think of Salesforce as the giant filing cabinet and switchboard for a company's customer relationships.

Now the filing cabinet has hired robot clerks (Agentforce) that answer the phones and do the paperwork themselves. Salesforce charges rent for the cabinet (per desk) — and is starting to also charge per task the robots complete.

brief.businessOverview:
Salesforce, Inc. (NYSE: CRM) is the world's largest customer-relationship-management (CRM) software company and the pioneer of cloud-delivered enterprise SaaS. Founded in 1999, it began as a sales-force-automation tool that proved business software could be delivered over the internet on a subscription, and has since expanded into a multi-cloud enterprise application suite — its 'Customer 360' / 'Data 360' platform — spanning sales, service, marketing, commerce, analytics, integration and collaboration. It generated record FY2026 revenue of ~$41.5B (year ended Jan 31, 2026) at a 34.1% non-GAAP operating margin, and is ranked #1 in CRM applications by worldwide market share (IDC).

Salesforce serves ~150,000+ customers across every industry and size, from small businesses to the bulk of the Fortune 500. Go-to-market combines a large direct enterprise sales force, a deep partner ecosystem (system integrators, ISVs, consulting partners), and the AppExchange marketplace. The model is land-and-expand: customers typically start in one cloud and add more over time, driving multi-cloud cross-sell. Ramp card-spend data corroborates the dominance — Salesforce captures ~70.7% of all CRM category spend across 50k+ businesses (rank #1).

Current strategy centers on AI 'digital labor.' Agentforce lets enterprises deploy autonomous AI agents for service, sales and other workflows, grounded by Data 360 (formerly Data Cloud), which unifies enterprise data so agents act on trusted, governed context. Management positions Agentforce + Data 360 as the fastest-growing products in company history — combined ARR reached ~$3.4B (+200%+ YoY) by Q1 FY2027, with Agentforce alone at $1.2B (+205% YoY).
brief.productModel:
Salesforce's architecture is a unified, multi-tenant cloud platform on which all applications share one metadata and data foundation. The core 'clouds' include Sales Cloud and Service Cloud (its two largest), Marketing and Commerce Cloud, the Platform (custom development, automation and Slack for collaboration), Data 360 (its customer-data platform), Tableau (analytics, 2019), MuleSoft (integration/APIs, 2018), Informatica (data management, 2025) and Agentforce (the agentic-AI layer). Slack (acquired for ~$27.7B in 2021) serves as the conversational front-end and 'agent operating system' for surfacing Agentforce inside the flow of work.

The revenue model is overwhelmingly recurring subscription & support (~95% of revenue) — per-seat term licenses billed annually in advance — which produces high visibility (reflected in RPO of $67.9B and cRPO of $33.6B, +14% YoY) and >90% subscription gross margins. With Agentforce, Salesforce is layering a consumption (usage-based) motion on top of seats: after initially pricing at ~$2 per conversation, it moved in May 2025 to 'Flex Credits,' where each Agentforce action consumes ~$0.10 of credits, sold in packs with pay-as-you-go and pre-commit options.

This seat-plus-consumption hybrid is the central business-model evolution: as AI agents do more 'work,' Salesforce monetizes actions/outcomes rather than only human seats, expanding addressable spend beyond headcount — but introducing lumpier, less predictable consumption revenue. As of Q1 FY2027 Salesforce also revised its disclosure to two subscription buckets — Agentforce Apps ($6.91B, 65% of subscription revenue, +9% YoY) and Data 360, Headless Platform & Other ($3.68B, 35%, +25% YoY) — retiring the old per-cloud splits. The ecosystem (AppExchange, system integrators, the Trailblazer developer community) extends the platform and is a major source of stickiness, increasingly important for rolling out Agentforce at enterprise scale.

recentNews.headline: Salesforce posts record Q1 FY27: revenue $11.13B (+13%), Agentforce ARR $1.2B (+205%), $25B accelerated buyback
recentNews.whyItMatters: Combined Agentforce + Data 360 ARR ~$3.4B (+200%+ YoY) validates the AI-monetization thesis, while a $25B ASR shrinks the share count as core growth normalizes toward ~11%.

recentNews.headline: Record Q4 FY26; $50B buyback authorization (largest ever) and FY27 revenue guide $45.8-46.2B
recentNews.whyItMatters: The $50B repurchase program (anchored by a $25B ASR) plus a 5.8% dividend increase mark a decisive capital-return pivot; FY27 guide of +10-11% and RPO $72.4B (+14%) frame the durable-growth case for the multiple.

recentNews.headline: Record Q3 FY26; FY26 guide raised, Agentforce + Data 360 ARR near $1.4B (+114% YoY)
recentNews.whyItMatters: Revenue $10.3B (+9%) at 35.5% non-GAAP op margin with FCF +22% kept the margin/cash story intact while triple-digit AI-product ARR growth gave the first hard proof Agentforce converts to recurring revenue.

recentNews.headline: Salesforce completes ~$8B acquisition of Informatica
recentNews.whyItMatters: Bolts enterprise data integration, governance and MDM onto the platform — the data backbone Agentforce needs — adding ~$1.1B Informatica Cloud ARR; its largest deal since Slack.

recentNews.headline: Salesforce expands partnerships with OpenAI and Anthropic; Agentforce coming to ChatGPT
recentNews.whyItMatters: Embedding Agentforce 360 into ChatGPT and naming Claude a preferred model for regulated industries widens distribution and de-risks the model layer — positioning Salesforce as an AI orchestrator rather than a single-LLM bet.

recentNews.headline: Agentforce 360 launches at Dreamforce 2025; Salesforce declares the 'Agentic Enterprise'
recentNews.whyItMatters: The rebranded flagship (Agentforce Builder, Agent Script, Voice, Intelligent Context) with 12,000 customers is the centerpiece of the AI re-rating narrative; proof points like Reddit deflecting 46% of support cases anchor the enterprise-ROI pitch.

recentNews.headline: Benioff confirms ~4,000 customer-support layoffs as Agentforce reduces headcount needs
recentNews.whyItMatters: Cutting support staff from ~9,000 to ~5,000 ('I need less heads') is both a margin tailwind and the clearest public demonstration of Salesforce eating its own AI — while raising the execution/optics risk of automating a core customer-facing function.

recentNews.headline: Salesforce signs definitive agreement to acquire Informatica for ~$8B equity value
recentNews.whyItMatters: Kicked off Salesforce's return to large-scale M&A after a buyback-focused, activist-pressured stretch — signaling management again sees data infrastructure as strategic enough to spend on rather than only return cash.

keyMetrics.agentAndAiArr: Agentforce ARR $1.2B (+205% YoY); Agentforce + Data 360 ~$3.4B (+200%+, incl. ~$1.1B Informatica) — Q1 FY27
keyMetrics.remainingPerfObligation: cRPO $33.6B (+14% YoY); total RPO $67.9B (+11% YoY) — as of Apr 30, 2026
keyMetrics.capitalReturn: $50B buyback authorization; ~$24.9B repurchased in Q1 FY27; dividend $0.44/qtr

earningsTakeaways.aiCommentary: Agentforce ARR crossed $1.2B, up 205% YoY — the fastest-scaling product line in company history and the centerpiece of the AI re-acceleration story. Combined Agentforce + Data 360 ARR reached ~$3.4B (+200%+ YoY), including ~$1.1B Informatica Cloud ARR. Bookings from premium SKUs (Agentforce One Edition, Agentforce for Apps) grew ~60% YoY. Usage signals underscored real consumption: 28.6T tokens processed (+152% QoQ) converted into 3.8B Agentic Work Units across Agentforce and Slack (+111% QoQ). Critically for durability, over 50% of Agentforce + Data 360 bookings came from existing customers expanding deployments — land-and-expand at higher consumption tiers rather than promotional land deals.
earningsTakeaways.demandCommentary: Demand was framed as healthy and broadening. A record 98 deals over $1M new ACV closed in Q1, with Slack reportedly ~half of the $1M+ wins. cRPO of $33.6B (+14% YoY) outpaced reported revenue growth (+13%), a constructive forward-demand signal, and >50% of Agentforce + Data 360 bookings were existing-customer expansions. CFO Robin Washington explicitly guided to organic revenue acceleration in 2H FY27 (Sales, Service, Slack, Agentforce, Data 360) — implying 1H is the trough of the transition year. The Ramp Data demand signal corroborates dominance but flags the bear case: Salesforce holds 70.7% of CRM category card-spend (rank #1) yet category adoption slipped 1.2% YoY with a ~43% competitor switch rate.

sources.name: Salesforce Investor Relations — Q1 FY2027 press release

SEC EDGAR — Salesforce 8-K Exhibit 99.1 (Q1 FY2027 earnings release)
Seeking Alpha — CRM Q1 FY2027 earnings call transcript

keyRisks: AI dis-intermediation of the seat model — Benioff's own ~4,000 support-role cuts show AI reducing headcount; if customers follow, per-seat licensing (the core revenue base) could erode faster than consumption revenue replaces it. The market has repeatedly refused to reward earnings beats on fears that 'software may not survive AI.'

Decelerated growth — total revenue grew ~13% in Q1 FY27 and FY27 guidance implies ~11%; Salesforce now sits in the low-growth tier of its comp set, closer to SAP (6%) and Adobe (12%) than to ServiceNow (22%), HubSpot (23%) or Oracle (22%), pressuring the case for multiple expansion.

Share erosion at the edges — Ramp data shows CRM category adoption -1.2% YoY and a ~43% competitor switch rate; HubSpot (mid-market), Microsoft Dynamics/Copilot and ServiceNow are all encroaching on different parts of the footprint.

Agentforce monetization is early and lumpy — the shift to consumption (Flex Credits) trades predictable seat revenue for usage-based revenue that is harder to forecast, and the ~$3.4B AI/Data ARR is a disclosed metric, not yet a clean P&L line.

Disclosure gaps — Salesforce does not disclose NRR/attrition, and it retired its per-cloud revenue splits as of Q1 FY27, reducing underwriting visibility during the most important transition in years.

M&A integration & capital-allocation optics — back-to-back large deals (Informatica ~$8B, Slack ~$27.7B) alongside a $50B buyback carry integration risk and the optics of repurchasing stock while automating staff.

comps.name: Salesforce
comps.note: subject; $186/sh, $152B mkt cap; 4.3x EV/Rev on 13% growth + 34.8% non-GAAP op margin (~Rule-of-48); $50B buyback authorization

comps.name: ServiceNow
comps.note: AI 'control tower' for enterprise workflows; premium 8.1x on 22% growth — the AI compounder CRM is measured against

comps.name: Microsoft
comps.note: Dynamics 365 + Copilot agents; 9.9x on 18% growth — the agent competitor carrying a far richer multiple

comps.name: Oracle
comps.note: Enterprise apps + OCI cloud; 11.5x on 22% growth — re-rated on AI/cloud capacity demand

comps.name: SAP
comps.note: Enterprise apps incumbent; 5.7x on 6% growth — the low-growth large-cap suite comp

comps.name: Adobe
comps.note: Creative/marketing software; 4.1x on 12% growth, ~89% GM — closest multiple comp to CRM

comps.name: Workday
comps.note: HR/Finance SaaS; 2.9x on 13.5% growth — similar growth, cheaper, narrower TAM

comps.name: HubSpot
comps.note: Mid-market CRM challenger; 2.9x on 23% growth — the downstream share-taker

swot.standoutSummary: Salesforce screens as the value name in large-cap software — 4.3x EV/Revenue and ~12x forward earnings vs ServiceNow 8.1x, Oracle 11.5x and Microsoft 9.9x — despite a comparable Rule-of-40 (13% growth + 34.8% non-GAAP margin ≈ 48) and the largest installed base in enterprise apps. The whole debate is whether Agentforce re-accelerates growth before AI compresses the per-seat model.

strengths.detail: ~150k customers and 70.7% of all CRM card-spend across 50k+ businesses (Ramp Data, rank #1) — an unmatched cross-sell surface for Agentforce.
34.8% non-GAAP operating margin and $6.6B quarterly free cash flow (Q1 FY27) — among the highest absolute FCF in software, funding a $50B buyback.
4.3x EV/Rev and ~12x forward P/E — a clear discount to NOW (8.1x), ORCL (11.5x) and MSFT (9.9x) for a profitable, 13%-growth megacap.

weaknesses.detail: 13% YoY vs NOW (22%), HUBS (23%), ORCL (22%) — Salesforce sits in the low-growth tier of its comp set, nearer SAP (6%) and ADBE (12%) than the AI compounders.
CRM category adoption -1.2% YoY and a ~43% competitor switch rate (Ramp Data) — share is being nibbled by HubSpot, Microsoft and ServiceNow.
No NRR/attrition disclosure and a revamped revenue taxonomy (per-cloud splits dropped in Q1 FY27) make the Agentforce transition harder to underwrite.

opportunities.detail: $1.2B ARR (+205% YoY) plus a consumption (Flex Credits) motion that monetizes AI 'actions' beyond seats — the key to re-rating toward peer multiples.
~$3.4B combined AI/data ARR; the unified data backbone deepens the moat and raises switching costs for agents.
$50B buyback / $25B ASR shrinks the share count into a normalizing-growth period, supporting double-digit EPS growth on single-digit-to-low-teens revenue growth.

threats.detail: The core bear case — if AI cuts customer headcount, per-seat licensing erodes faster than Agentforce consumption fills the gap.
Microsoft Copilot agents, ServiceNow, Sierra and the foundation-model players (OpenAI, Anthropic) all target the same enterprise agent budget.
Lumpier consumption revenue reduces the forecastability that historically supported Salesforce's multiple.

sources.name: yfinance / Yahoo Finance — live comps (2026-06-05 close)
Ramp Data — CRM category share & adoption

slideBullets: Value multiple in megacap software: 4.3x EV/Rev and ~12x forward P/E (2026-06-05 close) vs ServiceNow 8.1x, Oracle 11.5x, Microsoft 9.9x — the market is pricing CRM for sub-trend growth, not AI re-acceleration.
Q1 FY27 (ended Apr 30, 2026): revenue $11.13B (+13% YoY), non-GAAP operating margin 34.8% (+250 bps), non-GAAP EPS $3.88 (+50%), free cash flow $6.6B — a beat-and-raise on profitability.
Agentforce ARR $1.2B (+205% YoY); combined Agentforce + Data 360 ARR ~$3.4B (+200%+, incl. ~$1.1B Informatica) — the fastest-scaling product line in company history.
Capital return at scale: $50B buyback authorization, ~$24.9B repurchased in Q1 (incl. a $25B ASR); cRPO $33.6B (+14%) outpaces revenue — a constructive forward-demand signal.
Demand vs risk: 70.7% of CRM category card-spend and rank #1 adoption (Ramp Data) — but adoption -1.2% YoY and ~13% growth (vs 20%+ AI peers) frame the 'can software survive AI' debate.

Ramp Data — CRM category adoption trend

diligenceQuestions: What are net revenue retention and gross attrition doing inside the installed base — Salesforce no longer discloses NRR, and Ramp data shows CRM category adoption down 1.2% YoY with a ~43% switch rate?

How much of the ~$3.4B Agentforce + Data 360 ARR is net-new incremental spend versus repricing/rebundling of existing seat revenue — and what is the gross-margin profile of consumption (Flex Credits) revenue vs. subscription?
What organic, ex-Informatica, constant-currency subscription growth is embedded in the 2H FY27 'acceleration' guide, and what Agentforce attach/expansion rate is required to hit it?
